Weekly Docs Report

docs.sui.io Analytics

2026-04-27 to 2026-05-03 · Plausible + Kapa · Run 20260504T160324Z

Summary Traffic Top Pages Referrals Top Referrer URLs Page↔Theme Takeaways Topics User Engagement Sprint
Overview

Executive Summary

Kapa Questions
700
Themes Identified
12
Visitors (7d)
3,713
Weekday traffic peaks around 550–718 visitors/day; weekends drop to ~390–460, a ~30% decline typical of developer-focused docs.
Pageviews (7d)
9,396
Averages ~1,342 pageviews/day, with Apr 27 being the strongest day at 2,014 pageviews.
Bounce Rate (7d)
68%
Slightly higher than the 14d/30d rate of 66%, suggesting recent traffic may include more one-page-and-done visitors or search-driven single-intent lookups.
Views per Visit (7d)
2.3
Below the 14d average of 2.48, indicating recent visitors are engaging with fewer pages per session—worth monitoring.
Avg. Visit Duration (7d)
131 seconds
Lower than the 14d (143s) and 30d (153s) averages, suggesting the most recent cohort is spending less time per session.
Visitors (30d)
18,479
Healthy sustained monthly audience; the 30d views-per-visit (2.48) and duration (153s) are the strongest across all windows, suggesting longer-term visitors are more engaged.
This week, gRPC/GraphQL migration urgency dominated developer questions as the July 2026 JSON-RPC shutdown deadline approaches, while onboarding and package deployment pages drove the highest traffic on docs.sui.io. AI-referred visitors (ChatGPT, Gemini) showed disproportionately high engagement, signaling a growing AI-assisted developer workflow that warrants documentation optimization.
Plausible

Traffic Overview

Over the 7-day period (Apr 27–May 3, 2026), docs.sui.io attracted 3,713 visitors and 9,396 pageviews with a 68% bounce rate and average session duration of 131 seconds. Traffic is highest on weekdays and dips on weekends. The homepage and onboarding/install pages dominate traffic. Compared to the 14-day window (8,090 visitors), the second week (days 8–14) drove roughly 4,377 visitors, suggesting the most recent 7-day window is slightly below the prior-week pace. Direct traffic and Google are the top acquisition channels, with AI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini) beginning to appear as meaningful referrers.

Weekend Traffic Drop

Visitors fall sharply on weekends: May 2 saw only 389 visitors (lowest of the week) vs. 718 on Apr 27 (Monday). This ~46% weekend dip confirms a developer-dominated audience on professional schedules.

Peak Day Was Monday Apr 27

Apr 27 drove 2,014 pageviews—the weekly high—suggesting a post-weekend catch-up pattern where developers start the week by consulting docs.

Recent Engagement Decline vs. 30-Day Baseline

The 7d avg visit duration (131s) and views/visit (2.3) are meaningfully lower than the 30d averages (153s, 2.48), indicating a recent softening in session quality worth tracking.

AI Chatbot Referrals Emerging

ChatGPT and Gemini together sent ~23 visitors this week, but those visitors averaged 466s and 59 pageviews respectively—far above typical referral quality. As AI tools index docs more aggressively, this channel will grow.

Onboarding Funnel Health

Pages in the /getting-started/onboarding/* path show consistently low bounce rates (6–47%) and appear in sequence across top pages, confirming a healthy, multi-step developer onboarding funnel with strong continuation behavior.

DeepBook Content Over-Performs

The /onchain-finance/deepbookv3/deepbook page has a 15% bounce rate and deepbook.tech is the 5th largest referral source (88 visitors), pointing to an active external DeFi developer community engaging with DeepBook documentation.

API & Reference Pages Drive Single-Visit Lookups

Pages like /sui-api-ref (86% bounce) and /develop/accessing-data/graphql/graphql-rpc (93% bounce) show that reference content serves lookup-and-leave behavior—consider adding cross-links or navigation aids to reduce bounce.

Plausible

Top 20 Pages

# Page Insight
1 / Most visited page with 989 visitors and 1,242 pageviews; 54% bounce rate and 67s scroll depth indicate reasonable engagement for a landing page.
2 /getting-started/onboarding/sui-install 375 visitors, 512 pageviews; low 29% bounce rate and 54s scroll depth signal strong intent—users are reading and continuing their setup journey.
3 /sui-api-ref 143 visitors but high 86% bounce rate; users likely arrive for a quick API lookup and leave, consistent with reference doc behavior.
4 /onchain-finance/deepbookv3/deepbook 169 visitors, 212 pageviews with a very low 15% bounce rate—DeepBook content is highly engaging and driving multi-page sessions.
5 /sui-stack/zklogin-integration/zklogin 80 visitors, 170 pageviews with 47% bounce rate; strong pageview-to-visitor ratio (2.1x) shows users exploring zkLogin in depth.
6 /develop/sui-architecture/tokenomics-overview 136 visitors, 161 pageviews; moderate 54% bounce rate suggests a mix of casual browsers and focused readers.
7 /getting-started/tooling 106 visitors with 41% bounce rate; tooling page is a natural continuation from install, reflecting a healthy onboarding funnel.
8 /getting-started/onboarding 107 visitors with only 6% bounce rate—nearly all visitors continue to other pages, making this a strong funnel entry point.
9 /onchain-finance/tokenized-assets/create-nft 115 visitors, 13% bounce rate; NFT creation content retains users well, likely part of a multi-step tutorial flow.
10 /getting-started/onboarding/hello-world 99 visitors with 72% bounce rate; users may complete the hello-world exercise and exit, indicating task completion rather than disengagement.
11 /operators/full-node/sui-full-node 97 visitors, 52% bounce rate; operator content attracts a dedicated technical audience with moderate retention.
12 /getting-started/onboarding/configure-sui-client 76 visitors, 37% bounce rate; low bounce confirms users are progressing through the onboarding sequence.
13 /references/sui-api 97 visitors, 11% bounce rate—surprisingly low for a reference page, suggesting users are navigating deeply into the API docs.
14 /getting-started/onboarding/get-coins 91 visitors, 44% bounce rate; mid-funnel onboarding step with solid retention.
15 /getting-started 82 visitors, only 9% bounce rate; acts as a hub that consistently routes users deeper into the docs.
16 /references/fullnode-protocol 64 visitors, 12% bounce rate; technical protocol reference attracts engaged readers who explore further.
17 /getting-started/onboarding/local-network 48 visitors, 42% bounce rate; local network setup is a key developer step with reasonable engagement.
18 /getting-started/onboarding/ 70 visitors, 7% bounce rate; trailing-slash variant of the onboarding hub also performs as a strong funnel entry.
19 /onchain-finance/fungible-tokens/token-vesting-strategies 84 visitors, 11% bounce rate; token vesting content drives multi-page exploration, likely from DeFi developers.
20 /onchain-finance/asset-custody/address-balance-migration 59 visitors, 47% bounce rate; niche operator/finance content with moderate engagement, possibly driven by a specific migration need.
Plausible

Referral Sources

Source Type Insight
Direct / None Organic 1,880 visitors (largest single source); 77% bounce rate and only 92s duration suggest many direct visitors are quick-reference users or returning visitors who know exactly what they need.
Google Organic 847 visitors with 55% bounce rate and 218s duration—organic search brings high-quality, engaged visitors who stay significantly longer than direct traffic.
sui.io Organic 418 visitors referred from the main Sui website; 49% bounce rate and 193s duration indicate these are motivated users following deliberate CTAs.
Bing Organic 228 visitors with 87% bounce rate and only 64s duration; Bing traffic is largely low-quality with high single-page exits.
deepbook.tech Organic 88 visitors with 58% bounce rate and 61s duration; DeepBook ecosystem users arriving with specific protocol questions.
Baidu Organic 46 visitors with 92% bounce rate and only 4s duration; Baidu traffic is extremely low quality—likely bot or highly mismatched intent.
docs.sui.io Organic 39 self-referrals with 50% bounce rate and 326s duration; internal navigation from bookmarked or shared deep links, very high engagement time.
GitHub Organic 38 visitors from GitHub with 48% bounce rate and 139s duration; developer-sourced traffic from READMEs or issues is well-targeted.
blog.sui.io Organic 33 visitors from the Sui blog with 66% bounce rate and 74s duration; blog CTAs are driving docs visits but retention could improve.
chatgpt.com AI / Chatbot 9 visitors from ChatGPT with 56% bounce rate and 466s duration—AI-referred users spend the most time on site of any referral source, indicating deep research intent.
gemini.google.com AI / Chatbot 14 visitors from Gemini (via top_referrers) with 59 pageviews; AI assistant referrals are small but growing and show high engagement.
Twitter Organic 16 visitors with 62% bounce rate and 12s duration; social media traffic is low-intent and barely engages with docs content.
suiplay.sui.io Organic 16 visitors with 75% bounce rate and 9s duration; gaming-adjacent Sui product drives minimal and low-quality docs traffic.
Yahoo! Organic 4 visitors but 474s average duration—tiny sample but the longest session duration of any source, possibly a developer doing deep research.
coinglass.com Organic 8 visitors with 100% bounce rate and 0s duration; financial data site referrals have zero engagement—likely irrelevant traffic.
AI Referral Detail

chatgpt.com

9 visitors averaging 466 seconds on-site—the highest session duration of any referral source—indicating AI-referred users arrive with deep research intent and consume docs extensively.

gemini.google.com

14 visitors generating 59 pageviews; Gemini referrals outpace ChatGPT in volume and show similarly high multi-page engagement, confirming AI assistants are becoming a meaningful docs discovery channel.

Cross-Signal

Page ↔ Kapa Theme Correlations

High-traffic pages mapped to the Kapa themes they correspond to.

Page Related Kapa Theme Insight
/getting-started/onboarding/sui-install Package Publish & Deployment Low 29% bounce confirms install leads directly into deployment questions dominating Kapa.
/onchain-finance/deepbookv3/deepbook gRPC & GraphQL Data Access 15% bounce rate aligns with 88 deepbook.tech referrals and heavy gRPC migration questions.
/sui-api-ref gRPC & GraphQL Data Access 86% bounce reflects lookup-and-leave behavior matching 128 gRPC/GraphQL evidence questions.
/sui-stack/zklogin-integration/zklogin zkLogin & Cryptography 2.1x pageview-to-visitor ratio mirrors repeated Enoki email exposure questions in Kapa.
/getting-started/onboarding/hello-world Move Language & Standard Library 72% bounce suggests task completion but Move pattern questions spike immediately after.
/onchain-finance/tokenized-assets/create-nft Kiosk & NFT Patterns 13% bounce on NFT page aligns with sustained Kiosk/TransferPolicy confusion in Kapa.
/references/sui-api gRPC & GraphQL Data Access 11% bounce on API reference shows developers cross-navigating amid JSON-RPC deprecation.
/develop/sui-architecture/tokenomics-overview Coin & Token Standards 54% bounce matches mixed audience of DeFi builders and casual readers on token standards.
Analysis

Notable Takeaways

JSON-RPC Deprecation Is Driving a Question Surge

128 gRPC/GraphQL questions with 56 uncertain answers—the single largest uncertain theme this week

The July 2026 shutdown deadline is creating urgent migration confusion that the current docs are not fully resolving.

AI Chatbot Visitors Engage Far Longer Than Any Other Referral

ChatGPT-referred visitors averaged 466 seconds; the next highest organic source (Google) averaged 218 seconds

AI tools are sending highly motivated developers who read deeply, making docs quality disproportionately important for this channel.

Enoki zkLogin Email Exposure Generated a Cluster of Unanswered Questions

6 consecutive uncertain Kapa threads in the same session all asking variants of whether Enoki exposes OAuth email to the dApp

A single undocumented API behavior is generating repeated bot failures, suggesting the answer is missing entirely from the knowledge base.

DeepBook Content Retains Users at an Outlier Rate

15% bounce rate on /onchain-finance/deepbookv3/deepbook versus 68% sitewide average

An active external DeFi developer community is driving sustained multi-page sessions, warranting investment in DeepBook documentation depth.

Kapa

Developer Topics

This week saw 700+ questions across five chunk analyses, with the heaviest traffic around gRPC/GraphQL data access migration, package publishing/deployment, and PTB construction. Move language fundamentals, Kiosk/NFT patterns, Seal encryption, and zkLogin/cryptography also generated sustained developer confusion. Questions grouped by docs.sui.io category, then by specific topic.

Q: Is VecSet like tables, or like vectors? In terms of storage

Q: 所以UID只能通过内部， .uid 获得?无论对象是否共享？
A: 是的，总结得很准确。无论对象是**共享对象（shared object）**还是**私有对象**，获取其 `&UID` 的方式都是一样的： **必须通过对象所在模块提供的 accessor 方法**（如 `.uid()`），因为 `sui::object` 模块中的 `borrow_uid` 是私有函数，外部无法直接调用。[[Function borrow_uid](https://docs.s…

Move Package Structure No Workspace

Q: Does Move have workspaces?
A: Move does not have a "workspace" concept in the same way that Rust's Cargo does. Instead, Move organizes code into **packages**. A package is a directory containing a `Move.toml` manifest file and a `…
View thread →

Q: 什么是一个PTB
A: **PTB（Programmable Transaction Block，可编程交易块）** 是 Sui 上所有用户交易的基本执行单元。 ## 核心概念 PTB 是一组**有序命令**的集合，这些命令在输入上执行以定义交易结果。它允许用户在**单笔交易**中调用多个 Move 函数、管理对象和管理代币，而无需发布新的 Move 包。[[What is a PTB](https://docs.s…

TTO Unsupported Parent Object Types

1 certain · 0 uncertain
1 certain — spot check
Q: TTO 支持以下类型的父对象有上面说的4种，那不支持的类型有哪些？
A: 根据知识来源，TTO 机制对父对象的限制如下： **不支持作为父对象的类型：** - **Immutable（不可变对象）**：不可变对象无法作为父对象接收子对象。即使你将一个对象转移给不可变对象的 ID，也**无法访问**该子对象。 [[Owner enum](https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/blob/main/crates/sui-types/src/o…

Q: i need numbers with sign, which type

Q: getDynamicField的作用？
A: 您的问题与上一条相同，以下是对 `getDynamicField` 的总结： `getDynamicField` 用于**根据字段名称获取对象上的某个特定动态字段**。动态字段是可以在对象构建后动态添加或移除的字段，字段名可以是任意具有 `copy`、`drop`、`store` 能力的值。[[Dynamic Fields](https://docs.sui.io/guides/develope…

1 question

Package Immutability and UpgradeCap

1 certain · 0 uncertain
1 certain — spot check
Q: 为什么我在SUI的区块链浏览器里查看了几十个包，它们的Owner都是Immutable?意味着这些合约不可再进行升级了吗？
A: 这是 Sui 上包（Package）的正常现象，下面解释原因： ## 为什么包的 Owner 显示为 Immutable？ 在 Sui 上，**所有已发布的包（Package）本质上都是不可变（Immutable）对象**。这是 Sui 的设计机制：包一旦发布到链上，其字节码就永远不会改变。[[Upgrading Packages](https://docs.sui.io/develop/pu…
View thread →

1 certain — spot check
Q: sui move call 一个tx最多可以多少个？
A: 在 Sui 上，一个 PTB（Programmable Transaction Block）最多可以包含 **1,024 个**独立操作（commands）。 [[PTB 文档](https://docs.sui.io/develop/transactions/ptbs/prog-txn-blocks)] 也就是说，你可以在单个交易中链式调用最多 1,024 次 `MoveCall` 或其他命…

Q: 那为什么使用官方grpc端点可以正常转账而本地部署的grpc节点不行？
A: 这是一个很好的问题。根据知识库中的信息，本地部署的 gRPC 节点与官方节点行为不同，最可能的原因是**本地节点的 gRPC 索引未正确启用**。 ## 关键原因：本地节点需要显式启用 gRPC 索引 要在自部署的全节点上提供 gRPC 服务，必须在 `fullnode.yaml` 配置文件中启用 gRPC 索引： ```yaml rpc: enable-indexing: true `…

1 certain — spot check
Q: 怎么挖矿
A: 在 Sui 网络中，没有传统意义上的"挖矿"（PoW 工作量证明）机制。Sui 使用的是**委托权益证明（DPoS）**机制。您可以通过以下两种方式参与并获得收益： --- ## 方式一：质押 SUI 代币（Staking） 任何 SUI 持有者都可以将代币质押给验证者，从而获得质押奖励。 - 质押后，代币在整个 epoch（约 24 小时）内锁定。 - 新的质押请求在下一个 epoch …

Q: If they are in the same checkpoint, is it guaranteed the second transaction is ordered after the first? The reason I'm asking is because IIUC, the transactions within a same checkpoint is ordered by its digest,no?

1 question

Full Node State Inconsistency & Snapshot Recovery

1 certain · 0 uncertain
1 certain — spot check
Q: 并不是落后checkpoint，而是同步后和链上状态不一致，那么我觉得应该是某个接受的checkpoint数据有问题，那么怎么快速恢复呢
A: 你的判断很有道理——如果节点已经同步到最新 checkpoint，但本地状态与链上不一致，很可能是某个 checkpoint 数据在接收或执行时出现了问题，导致本地数据库处于不一致状态。 根据知识库中的信息，**最快的恢复方式仍然是从快照重建**，因为这可以绕过有问题的本地数据库状态，直接恢复到一个经过协议验证的干净状态。 ## 推荐：使用 Formal Snapshot 恢复 Formal…
View thread →

Q: 什么意思 什么叫sui生态所有钱包？

SUI Token Decimals and MIST

1 certain · 0 uncertain
1 certain — spot check
Q: SUI 精度是多少？
A: SUI 的精度为 **9 位小数**。 最小单位称为 **MIST**，1 SUI = 1,000,000,000 MIST（即 10⁹ MIST）。[[sui::sui 模块](https://docs.sui.io/references/framework/sui_sui/sui)] 在 Sui TypeScript SDK 中，也提供了相关常量方便开发者使用：[[SDK 工具函数](ht…

Sprint Planning

Recommended Actions

Doc Actions
high Documentation ✓ Confirmed

Publish a JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL Migration Guide with Side-by-Side Code

Create a single canonical migration page covering endpoint changes, SuiGrpcClient setup, pagination field renames, and the July 2026 timeline.

128 questions with 56 uncertain answers and multiple threads asking the same endpoint URLs weekly; deadline pressure is accelerating confusion.

↑ Resolve the top uncertain-answer theme and reduce repeat questions that are currently overwhelming the chatbot.
The docs have separate gRPC and GraphQL pages (https://docs.sui.io/develop/accessing-data/grpc/index.md, https://docs.sui.io/develop/accessing-data/graphql/index.md) but no single canonical migration guide covering JSON-RPC to gRPC/GraphQL endpoint changes, SuiGrpcClient setup, pagination renames, and a July 2026 timeline side-by-side.
high Documentation ✓ Confirmed

Add Enoki zkLogin OAuth Identity Exposure Reference

Document exactly which fields (label, icon, email) the Enoki wallet account exposes via useCurrentAccount and how to access the OAuth subject server-side.

Six consecutive uncertain Kapa threads in one session all failed on this exact undocumented behavior.

↑ Eliminate a cluster of unanswerable bot questions and unblock OAuth-gated dApp developers.
No dedicated page documenting Enoki zkLogin OAuth identity field exposure (label, icon, email) via useCurrentAccount or server-side OAuth subject access is present in the listed pages.
high Documentation ✓ Confirmed

Expand PTB Cookbook with Coin Selection, Sponsored Flows, and DEX Patterns

Add runnable PTB examples for coinWithBalance, fragmented coin merging, sponsored transactions, DEX swap patterns, and gas estimation.

78 questions with 34 uncertain answers span coin selection, vector inputs, and DEX abort prevention—all lacking concrete examples.

↑ Reduce the second-largest uncertain theme and directly support active DeFi builders using DeepBook and Cetus.
There is a PTB CLI reference page (https://docs.sui.io/references/cli/ptb.md) but no cookbook with runnable examples for coinWithBalance, coin merging, sponsored transactions, DEX swap patterns, or gas estimation.
Tooling Actions
high Tooling ✓ Confirmed

Improve SuiGrpcClient Error Messages for JSON-RPC Endpoint Misuse

Add a clear runtime error when a JSON-RPC URL is passed to SuiGrpcClient as baseUrl, and improve the 'Transaction resolution is not supported' message to suggest the correct client.

Multiple Kapa threads show developers silently misconfiguring the gRPC client with RPC endpoints and receiving cryptic failures during migration.

↑ Reduce migration support burden and unblock developers who currently lose hours to misconfiguration.
No documentation exists covering SuiGrpcClient error messages for JSON-RPC endpoint misuse or improving the 'Transaction resolution is not supported' message.
medium Tooling ✓ Confirmed

Add Move Compiler Warnings for Deprecated Coin Standard Usage

Emit a compiler warning when code uses coin::create_currency or the deprecated DenyCap v1 pattern instead of the new coin_registry::new_currency API.

Developers are unknowingly publishing tokens with deprecated patterns, discovering the issue only at runtime or via Kapa questions.

↑ Prevent a category of silent correctness bugs before deployment and reduce currency standard migration questions.
While coin_registry is referenced in the framework docs (https://docs.sui.io/references/framework/sui_sui/coin_registry.md), there is no documentation of Move compiler warnings for deprecated coin::create_currency or DenyCap v1 usage.
DX Actions
high Developer Experience ✓ Confirmed

Add a sui client grpc-check Diagnostic Command

Build a new CLI subcommand that verifies a given endpoint supports gRPC, lists available services, and reports whether the node has indexing enabled.

Developers cannot distinguish between gRPC-capable and JSON-RPC-only endpoints, causing silent failures that generate the most uncertain Kapa answers this week.

↑ Give every developer a self-service migration validation tool and reduce the most urgent uncertain-answer theme.
The CLI reference (https://docs.sui.io/references/cli/client.md) does not include any 'sui client grpc-check' diagnostic subcommand in the listed documentation.
medium Developer Experience ✓ Confirmed

Build a zkLogin Session Inspector CLI Command

Create a new sui keytool zklogin-inspect command that decodes a zkLogin session, shows the ephemeral key expiry, current epoch, maxEpoch bound, and JWK kid status.

Developers cannot inspect zkLogin session state without custom tooling, causing repeated JWK rotation failures and maxEpoch confusion questions.

↑ Unblock zkLogin integration debugging and reduce the cluster of unanswerable cryptography questions around session and JWK state.
The keytool CLI reference (https://docs.sui.io/references/cli/keytool.md) is listed but no 'sui keytool zklogin-inspect' command for decoding zkLogin sessions and inspecting ephemeral key expiry is documented.
docs.sui.io Docs Report · 2026-04-27 to 2026-05-03 · Plausible + Kapa · Run 20260504T160324Z
